title: Measurement Terms
description: Measurement and verification terms for media buys. Appears on products (seller defaults), package requests (buyer proposals), and confirmed packages (agreed terms). All fields are optional — presence indicates the term is declared or proposed.

billing_measurement.description: Which vendor's count of the billing metric governs invoicing. The billing metric is determined by the pricing_model on the selected pricing_option (e.g., impressions for CPM, completed views for CPCV).

vendor.description: Vendor whose measurement of the billing metric is authoritative for invoicing (e.g., { domain: 'campaignmanager.google.com' } for buyer's DCM, { domain: 'admanager.google.com' } for seller's GAM).

max_variance_percent.description: Maximum acceptable variance between the billing vendor's count and the other party's count before resolution is triggered (e.g., 10 means a 10% divergence triggers review).

ivt_threshold.description: Invalid Traffic threshold and the vendor responsible for measuring it.

max_percent.description: Maximum acceptable IVT percentage (e.g., 5 means less than 5% invalid traffic). Measured by the specified vendor.

vendor.description: Vendor responsible for IVT detection and measurement (e.g., { domain: 'doubleverify.com' }). When specified on a confirmed package, creatives assigned to this package MUST include tracker_script or tracker_pixel assets from this vendor.

viewability.description: Viewability threshold, measurement standard, and the vendor responsible for measuring it.

min_percent.description: Minimum viewability percentage (e.g., 70 means at least 70% of impressions must be viewable).

vendor.description: Vendor measuring viewability (e.g., { domain: 'integralads.com' }). When specified on a confirmed package, creatives assigned to this package MUST include corresponding tracker assets from this vendor.
